description: Public opinion in Japan regarding the 2005 Supreme Court rulings that held the government liable for the Minamata disease mercury poisoning tragedy, which began in the 1950s. The debate centered on the extent of government responsibility versus the chemical corporation's liability.

key_events.description: Japan's Supreme Court issues a landmark ruling affirming government negligence.
Nationwide media coverage highlights the struggles of unrecognized victims.
The Ministry of the Environment announces a new comprehensive medical relief policy.
National consensus shifts toward prioritizing historical reparations over budget constraints.

calibration_notes: Useful for analyzing long-tail health crises where public sentiment evolves over decades from denial to demand for restorative justice.
